name: entry_base_zero_idx_not_base
code:
proof fn phi_1_entry_base_zero_idx_not_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, 0) != base,
{
}
reason: entry_base at index 0 should equal the base address; if this proves, the address calculation is fundamentally broken

name: entry_base_not_strictly_increasing
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_not_strictly_increasing(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i + 1 < arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, (i + 1) as nat) <= arch.entry_base(layer, base, i),
{
}
reason: consecutive entry bases must be strictly increasing for entries to map disjoint regions; provability implies overlapping or reversed address layout

name: inv_page_forced_zero_size
code:
proof fn phi_3_inv_page_forced_zero_size(d: Directory, i: nat)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        i < d.entries.len(),
        d.entries[i as int] is Page,
    ensures
        d.entries[i as int]->Page_0.frame.size == 0,
{
}
reason: under inv, page frame sizes must equal entry_size which is positive; if this proves, the invariant erroneously forces zero-size frames

name: empty_dir_has_mapping
code:
proof fn phi_4_empty_dir_has_mapping(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        d.empty(),
    ensures
        exists|k: nat| d.interp().dom().contains(k),
{
}
reason: an empty directory should have no mappings; provability means the interp of an all-Invalid directory is non-empty, violating the empty semantics

name: child_entry_size_exceeds_parent
code:
proof fn phi_5_child_entry_size_exceeds_parent(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat) > arch.entry_size(i),
{
}
reason: parent layer entries cover larger regions than children (entry_size(i) = entry_size(i+1) * num_entries(i+1)); provability means the size hierarchy is inverted
